summary: App shell exposes the route container, the storage adapter, the navigation callback, and the archetype roster registry to Table mode.

route_registration.name: registerRoute
route_registration.shape: (routeId: string, mountFn: (container: HTMLElement) => void, unmountFn?: () => void) => void
route_registration.purpose: Table mode registers itself under routeId='table' at boot.

storage_adapter.name: storage
storage_adapter.shape: { get(key: string): any|null, set(key: string, value: any): void, clear(prefix?: string): void }
storage_adapter.namespace_rule: Table mode persists state under prefix 'gto-trainer/v1/table/' (e.g., session id, hero stack, seat arrangement).

archetype_registry.name: getArchetypes
archetype_registry.purpose: Table mode reads the roster loaded by section-4 at boot to populate the 8 non-hero seats.

data_passed_not_logic: Shell passes a DOM container, function references, and a registry getter. Shell does not implement any gameplay; table does not implement shell-level concerns.

section-1_owns: registerRoute, storage adapter, navigateHome, archetype registry getter
section-5_owns: all gameplay state and persistence under its prefix

test_seam: An integration test mounts Table mode into a stub container and asserts that getArchetypes() returns a non-empty roster.
